reasoning: The conversion failed to capture the primary utility of the document: the pricing tables. Multiple sections (Sophos, Trellix, CrowdStrike) have tables truncated from dozens of rows down to one or two, or missing entirely where content was rendered as images. While programmatic recall is high, the structural and visual fidelity scores are poor, indicating the document's core data is unusable in its current state.

concerns.analysis:
Several tables in the output are severely truncated compared to the source. 
1. Section 4 ('Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List Part 1') contains only 2 data rows, whereas the source tables on pages 13, 14, and 15 contain a combined 43 rows of data. 
2. Section 5 ('DLT CrowdStrike Cost Proposal Form Table') contains only 1 data row, while source page 16 has 9 rows of cost items. 
3. Section 6 ('SHI CrowdStrike Price List Table') contains only 1 data row, while source page 18 has 8 row
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Source pages 13-15 (Carahsoft Trellix table with 43 rows); Page 16 (DLT CrowdStrike table with 9 rows); Page 18 (SHI CrowdStrike table with 8 rows); Page 19 (Presidio Cisco table with 9 rows).
concerns.output_quote: Section 4 Table (2 rows); Section 5 Table (1 row); Section 6 Table (1 row); Section 7 Table (1 row).

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis:
There are major text and data omissions across several sections where the pipeline failed to extract structured content, providing only image placeholders. 
1. Section 2 (Sophos) contains only headers and images for pages 5 through 9 of the source, which consist of extensive pricing tables. None of this data was converted into text or table elements. 
2. Section 3 (SHI Trellix) is missing the structured data for the 'Maintenance costs', 'Training', and 'Installation' tables found on source page
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Source pages 5-9 (Sophos pricing tables); Source page 11 (Trellix maintenance, training, and installation tables).
concerns.output_quote: Sections 2 and 3 do not contain structured table or text data for these pages, only headers and images.

concerns.analysis: Multiple tables throughout the document are severely truncated in the conversion. The structural representation of these tables in the JSON is corrupted because the vast majority of the rows present in the source are missing from the table objects. 1. The Carahsoft Trellix table (index 15) contains only 2 data rows (Line 10 and 30) out of the 43 line items shown in the source (Pages 13-15). 2. The DLT CrowdStrike table (index 20) contains only 1 data row out of the 10 rows present in the source
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The table on Page 17 (Cisco) includes rows for 'Cloud Subscription', 'Customization', 'Additional Modules', 'Technical End User Doc', 'Installation', 'Technical Support', 'Training', 'Maintenance', and 'Other Costs'.
concerns.output_quote: The corresponding table at index 30 only contains the first data row ('Cloud Subscription/License Fee').
concerns.location: Presidio Cisco Cost Proposal Form, Page 19, index 30

concerns.analysis: The visual organization of the Sophos and SHI Trellix sections is significantly compromised. In the source PDF, these sections (pages 3-9) consist of detailed pricing tables. In the converted HTML, these sections are rendered as a sequence of headings with no content (tables) between them. This fundamentally changes the layout and prevents a user from finding the primary information in the expected location.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The SOPHOS section (PDF pages 3-7) and the SHI Trellix section (PDF pages 8-9) are composed of multiple detailed tables providing pricing and product information.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output for the SOPHOS and SHI Trellix sections contains only nested headings (e.g., 'Cloud Subscription/Licensing Fee', 'Professional Services and Additional Modules') with zero content or tables rendered under them.

concerns.analysis: The 'Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List (Continued)' table is visually broken due to missing data. In the source PDF (pages 11-13), this is a continuous list of items from Line 1 to Line 43. The HTML output renders a table that only includes Line 10 and Line 30, skipping all other 41 rows. This makes the table incomplete and unusable for a sighted user looking for the full price list.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List spanning PDF pages 11, 12, and 13, containing lines 1 through 43.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML table titled 'Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List (Continued)' only contains two data rows: Line 10 and Line 30.
concerns.location: Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List (Continued) section

concerns.analysis: Multiple pages in the source PDF (3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 15) contain essential pricing information rendered as images of tables or forms. These meaningful images are completely absent from the HTML output, and there are no equivalent text or image elements representing this critical content.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Visual tables on PDF pages 3, 4, 5, 6 (Sophos), 8, 9 (SHI Trellix), and 15 (DLT CrowdStrike) are images containing all relevant pricing data for those sections.
concerns.output_quote: No image elements () or converted text data corresponding to these visual tables are present in the HTML screenshot.
concerns.location: SOPHOS, SHI Trellix, and DLT (CrowdStrike) sections

concerns.analysis: A URL is clearly visible on Page 1 of the PDF (Symantec training). While the text of the URL is captured correctly in the JSON (index 3, row 13), it is not converted into a functional, clickable hyperlink object, representing a minor loss of structural/interactive fidelity.
concerns.location: CARAHSOFT (BROADCOM CA, INC.) SYMANTEC PRICE LIST, Page 3, index 3

Ensure image-based pricing forms are transcribed into accessible HTML table elements.
Check for missing rows in all table objects compared to the source PDF.
